message:
feat(secrets): Cx-first scan_storage + scan_storage_incremental (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Expand the Cx-first surface into secrets.rs. The `SecretScanEngine`
drives a batch loop across stored segments; without cx threading, a
long-running scan ignores caller cancellation.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `SecretScanEngine::scan_storage_with_cx(cx, storage, options)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `scan_storage`. Delegates to the private
    `scan_storage_from_with_cx`.

  * `SecretScanEngine::scan_storage_incremental_with_cx(cx, storage,
    options)` — Cx-first parallel of `scan_storage_incremental`.
    Checkpoint before touching storage (pre-cancelled cx never hits
    the DB), then routes the batch scan through the Cx-first
    internal helper, then writes the scan-report record via the
    (still ambient) `storage.record_secret_scan_report`.

  * `SecretScanEngine::scan_storage_from_with_cx(cx, storage,
    options, resume_after_id)` — private Cx-first scan helper.
    Mirrors `scan_storage_from` exactly, but adds:
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` at the top (pre-cancelled cx returns
        empty report).
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` at the start of each batch iteration,
        embedding the current `after_id` in the error message so a
        cancellation surface from any iteration carries its
        resume-offset info.

Per-iteration semantics: if cx is cancelled mid-scan, the loop
exits with an Error::Runtime carrying the `after_id` of the last
committed batch. The caller can capture that and resume via
`scan_storage_from_with_cx(..., Some(last_after_id))` on a fresh
cx — the Cx-first path preserves the full incremental-resume
contract.

The legacy `scan_storage_from`, `scan_storage`, and
`scan_storage_incremental` remain untouched and continue to route
through the non-cx path. No existing caller's behavior changes.

Regression test (new)
`scan_storage_with_cx_matches_legacy_with_matches`:

  * Seeds a StorageHandle with 3 segments (2 containing secrets:
    OPENAI_API_KEY, GH_TOKEN; 1 without).
  * Runs BOTH `scan_storage(&storage, options)` and
    `scan_storage_with_cx(&cx, &storage, options)` with a fresh
    `Cx::for_request()`.
  * Asserts parity across all deterministic fields:
    scanned_segments, scanned_bytes, matches_total, samples.len(),
    report_version, last_segment_id.
  * Asserts `matches_total >= 2` to confirm the Cx-first scan
    actually exercised the pattern matcher (not just an empty
    no-op).

`completed_at` is explicitly excluded from parity assertions
because the two calls run sequentially with a few ms between them;
every other field is bit-for-bit equivalent.

Follow-on: once `StorageHandle::scan_segments` gains a `_with_cx`
variant, thread cx *into* the query so cancellation reaches the
SQLite driver. Today the seam is between batches only.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * secrets:: — 72 passed / 0 failed (tick 50: 71 -> tick 51: 72,
    +1 new parity test; all 4 legacy scan_storage tests still
    green, proving the strictly-additive guarantee)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
